headlines.title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo
headlines.description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Deadly shooting at San Diego Islamic center
headlines.description: Police said two teenage gunmen opened fire at a San Diego Islamic center, killing three people, including a security guard. Authorities said the gunmen later killed themselves. NBC News' Morgan Chesky reports.

headlines.title: Trump Targets Kentucky Rep. Thomas Massie in Tuesday Primary
headlines.description: Voters are heading to the polls for primaries across six states, including Kentucky, where the campaign of Rep. Thomas Massie, a repeated Trump critic, is being seen as a crucial test of President Donald Trump’s power on the Republican party. Meanwhile, the Justice Department set up what it calls an “anti-weaponization fund,” using nearly $1.8 billion of taxpayer money to pay Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports and

headlines.title: Are consumers cracking under the weight of high prices? We’re about to find out.
headlines.description: Home Depot, Lowe’s, Walmart and Target report earnings this week, offering a clear picture of how consumers are faring amid inflation and high energy costs.

headlines.title: Goodbye Trump, hello Putin: China welcomes Russian leader facing pressure at home
headlines.description: Vladimir Putin landed in China on Tuesday to a warm welcome from his “dear friend” President Xi Jinping, but he arrived with the heavy burden of a war he increasingly seems unable to win.

headlines.title: Trump says he called off a planned attack on Iran
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said he is calling off an attack on Iran planned for Tuesday because regional leaders had urged him to allow negotiations to continue and a “very acceptable” deal for the U.S. was at hand.

headlines.title: First look at new Trump Mobile smartphone
headlines.description: After a nine-month wait, repeated calls and emails, NBC News finally received the long-delayed Trump Mobile T1 phone - raising new questions about where it’s actually made. NBC News’ Brian Cheung reports.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Goodbye Trump, hello Putin: China welcomes Russian leader facing pressure at home
headlines.description: Vladimir Putin landed in China on Tuesday to a warm welcome from his “dear friend” President Xi Jinping, but he arrived with the heavy burden of a war he increasingly seems unable to win.

headlines.title: ‘Heroic’ security guard killed protecting children and staff in San Diego mosque shooting
headlines.description: More than $1.4 million has been raised for the family of Amin Abdullah, the security guard fatally shot on Monday at a San Diego mosque while protecting the lives of more than a dozen children and staff at a school inside

headlines.title: Ebola deaths rise sharply as ‘scale and speed’ of outbreak concerns WHO
headlines.description: The death toll from an Ebola outbreak in central Africa rose sharply Tuesday, with the head of the World Health Organization expressing concern over the “scale and speed of the epidemic.”At least 131 people are believed to have been killed and 531 suspected to have been infected in the latest outbreak, according to the Congolese Ministry of Health.

headlines.title: Trump loyalty tests, major battlegrounds and more: What to watch in Tuesday’s elections
headlines.description: President Donald Trump’s campaign to suppress dissent in his own party faces its most prominent test yet as he takes aim at Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky, while GOP candidates across the country look to leverage the president’s endorsement in key primaries.
